retrieved_note: Pilot extraction started from weak public references, but the MCB Visa Signature Debit Card record is now supplemented with the official MCB Signature FAQ PDF plus later surfaced SOBC mirrors.

notes: Official LinkedIn indicates this is an active debit-card product.
Later official post states ATM withdrawals up to PKR 350,000/day and POS/online spending up to PKR 800,000/day.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: Conditional fee structure. Latest surfaced Jan-Jun 2026 SOBC mirror shows PKR 10,000 per quarter charged only if quarterly average total deposits stay below PKR 5 million and quarterly average current-account balance stays below PKR 2 million. Earlier 2025 mirrors showed PKR 5,000 per quarter under the same balance-waiver structure.

notes: Official LinkedIn indicates the card is actively marketed.
Official MCB FAQ PDF confirms deposit-based eligibility rather than salary-based eligibility.
Current public evidence found in the official FAQ PDF and later surfaced SOBC mirrors supports a balance-based eligibility and a conditional quarterly fee rather than a flat published annual fee.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: Free for Sohni Dharti Remittance Current Account customers.

notes: Official LinkedIn states SDRA includes a free PayPak Debit Card.

gaps: The official MCB Signature FAQ PDF was recovered later, but most other MCB cards still do not have strong MCB-controlled public documentation captured in the repo.
Named cards like MCB Visa Gold Credit Card, MCB Visa Platinum Credit Card, MCB Visa Debit Card, and MCB GOLD PLUS were referenced publicly but not with detailed requirement tables.
MCB Visa Platinum Debit Card and generic PayPak Debit Card records still need stronger fee and balance evidence.
